qa_pairs.question: What are the primary types of floating-point operations that roughly half of the users utilize?
qa_pairs.answer: Roughly half of the users primarily utilize single-precision floating-point operations (FP32), while the other half use half-precision floating-point operations (FP16).

qa_pairs.question: What proportion of users are constrained by GPU memory?
qa_pairs.answer: A significant portion of all users are constrained by the amount of memory on the GPU.

qa_pairs.question: What factors led to the selection of evaluation criteria for ranking GPU models?
qa_pairs.answer: Evaluation criteria and corresponding weights were chosen to rank different GPU models because roughly half of the users primarily use single-precision floating-point operations (FP32), the other half use half-precision floating-point operations (FP16), and a significant portion of all users are constrained by the amount of memory on the GPU.

qa_pairs.question: Which floating-point formats are mentioned as being primarily used by users?
qa_pairs.answer: The floating-point formats mentioned as being primarily used by users are single-precision (FP32) and half-precision (FP16).
